title: rules.rs duplicates extension/language registry across three constants
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/rules.rs:11-56 (extension_to_language), 147-208 (parse_rule_name, known_languages)
## Violation
The mapping between source extensions and language names is hard-coded in TWO independent constants that must stay in sync:

```rust
fn extension_to_language(ext: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    match ext.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "rs" => Some("rust"),
        ...
}
...
let known_languages = [
    "rust", "python", "javascript", ...
];
```

Plus a third partial copy in `auto_learn.rs::has_source_extension` / `has_file_extension` (hard-coded `"rs"|"py"|"ts"|"js"`). Adding a new language requires three coordinated edits or the rules engine silently degrades. The two arrays disagree today on at least `"sql"` and `"shell"` (in `known_languages` but with a different ext‚Üílang mapping in `extension_to_language` ‚Äî `sql` maps to `sql`, `sh` maps to `shell`).

The string‚Üílanguage match also uses `.to_lowercase()` (allocates a `String` on every lookup) instead of `.eq_ignore_ascii_case()` (no alloc).
## Mandated refactor
Define ONE `const LANGUAGE_REGISTRY: &[(&str, &[&str])]` mapping a canonical language name to its known extensions. Derive `extension_to_language` and `known_languages` as lazily-built `HashMap` / `HashSet` from this single source. Make `auto_learn.rs` consume the same registry via `pub use rules::is_source_extension`. Remove the `to_lowercase` allocation in favor of byte-wise case-insensitive comparison.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
